//: Rosetta binding manifest for the Geogram library.
//paths: All relative paths resolve from THIS file's directory (the project root).
//design: GEO::Mesh is bound DIRECTLY, and so are its member objects: mesh.vertices / mesh.facets / mesh.facet_corners are member-object reference properties (non-copyable stores holding a Mesh& back-reference; 'final': true suppresses their trampolines — MeshVertices/MeshFacets have public virtuals — which is also what lets node alias them). Geometry moves through geogram's own API: vertices.assign_points / facets.assign_triangle_mesh / vertices.point_coordinates, whose GEO::vector<T> parameters marshal as flat script arrays via the 'sequences' trait (their raw-pointer overloads are declared second, so the walk keeps the sequence one). Only TWO extension functions remain (src/mesh_ext.h): triangles() and tex_coords(), which encode the fan-triangulation-of-polygons output policy. load()/save() are Mesh members upstream (non-overloaded, PR #373) and bind natively, like copy/clear/show_stats. The GEO:: algorithms (remesh, repair, Co3Ne reconstruction, atlas, intersections) are bound straight from their headers; georo:: keeps only initialize(), the overloaded boolean helpers and the shared_ptr-returning CSG entry points (src/algorithms.h). NOTE: rosetta does not capture C++ default arguments — every parameter is explicit in the bindings.
//sources: The geogram sources are compiled straight into every binding target (no prebuilt libgeogram: the wasm target needs the sources anyway, and a native lib built by another toolchain would risk an ABI mismatch). One glob per source dir, DELIBERATELY not the recursive ** — the set is selective: geogram/lua/, geogram/api/ and most of third_party/ (tetgen, triangle, PoissonRecon, stb, amgcl, lua) must stay out. The vendored zlib/rply/libMeshb/OpenNL are C sources (the generated projects enable_language(C)).
//defaults: The top-level namespace default qualifies every unqualified class/function name with GEO:: (georo::* spellings pass verbatim). Groups ({"header": ..., "entries": [...]}) scope a shared header over a run of entries.
//defines: GEOGRAM_USE_BUILTIN_DEPS: mesh_io/geofile include the vendored libMeshb/rply/zlib instead of system libs. GEOGRAM_WITH_HLBFGS: compiles the HLBFGS optimizer in (required by the Newton iterations of CVT remeshing).
//version-h: src/geogram/version.h stands in for the header geogram's own CMake would generate; src/ is listed before extern/geogram/src/lib so it wins the include lookup.
//link-options: Under Node, geogram's GEO::initialize() mounts the host filesystem with NODEFS (EM_ASM in basic/common.cpp), so the wasm link needs emscripten's nodefs.js library — exactly what geogram's own cmake/platforms/Emscripten-clang.cmake adds.
//sequences: GEO::vector<T> marshals like std::vector<T> (flat script arrays) through rosetta's sequence adapters — this is what lets geogram's own assign_points / assign_triangle_mesh bind (their GEO::vector overload is declared first, so it survives the walk's overload dedup and the adapter resolves the call by name). Mutable GEO::vector& parameters bind input-only (steal_arg steals from the adapter's copy, which is fine).

classes.//: --- the mesh and its member-object stores ---

functions.//: --- booleans (georo glue: GEO::mesh_union et al. are overloaded) ---
